items.rationale: He is most actively deploying capital here — leading a $40M EVE Universe investment and a Proof of Play seed round — and frames composability, player-owned assets, modding, and autonomous worlds as the future of games.

citations.quote: a16z is leading a $40M investment in @CCPGames' new AAA gaming project set in the EVE Universe
Excited to announce that we're leading a seed investment in @ProofOfPlay, an on-chain game studio and tech company
players own their assets, and anyone can create their own game on top of another

items.rationale: His core thesis, crystallized in his book Read Write Own: blockchains are the only plausible path to sustaining an open internet that shifts power from Big Tech to users and rewards creators/entrepreneurs.

citations.quote: crypto/web3 is the only plausible path to sustaining the original vision of the internet as an open platform
realign the future of the Internet away from Big Tech to the people who use it

items.rationale: Announced a new investment in Gensyn (decentralized ML compute) and lists AI among the intersecting subjects he explores in his book — signaling interest in the crypto-AI overlap.
items.age_note: Supported mainly by a single June 2023 deal announcement plus a book reference; not repeatedly emphasized.

citations.quote: today we announced our newest investment in UK-based @gensynai
I also go deeper into intersecting subjects like AI, social networks, finance, media businesses, collaborative creation

items.rationale: Personally enthusiastic about Apple Vision Pro, expecting VR to follow prior computing platforms in getting cheaper/faster/lighter as Meta and Apple invest. Adjacent to, not part of, his crypto thesis.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2023-06-05 and never restated through year-end; treat as a standing-but-stale enthusiasm.

citations.quote: Excited for Vision Pro. With Meta and now Apple investment, VR will follow the same pattern as past computing platforms

items.rationale: He repeatedly distances his builder thesis from the speculative, casino-like culture around crypto and wants it stamped out so the industry can meet its promise.

citations.quote: stamping out the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto
I tackle controversial topics like policy/regulatory, and the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto

items.rationale: His dominant late-2023 concern: lack of clear US market-structure rules raises compliance costs for honest startups, and some policymakers want to ban crypto outright — prompting his political/PAC engagement.
items.age_note: Persistent theme March–December 2023; most recent and sharpest framing is 2023-12-18.

citations.quote: certain policymakers believe it should be banned, while other people think it should have no guardrails. Neither of those options will allow the technology to reach its full potential
Crypto/web3 is still in the early innings, and needs clear regulations to provide an open pathway for startups

items.rationale: He worries good-faith, compliant companies get undercut or taken out by unscrupulous competitors (citing FTX and Binance) absent clear rules and enforcement.

citations.quote: Competitors like FTX and Binance were taking market share and rapidly introducing new products in ways that... exceeded... the boundaries of the law
some will get taken out by unscrupulous competitors along the way, or will get overwhelmed with legal and compliance costs

risk_regime.rationale: Dixon is a structural long-term builder/investor in crypto and web3 — actively leading new deals (gaming, decentralized AI), publishing a thesis book, expanding internationally, and repeatedly stating crypto is in 'early innings.' His risk-on posture is tempered (not reversed) by near-term regulatory and fraud concerns, which he meets by pushing for clearer rules rather than retreating.

notes: View is built from public X statements Feb–Dec 2023; many posts are firm/portfolio promotion, so individual conviction is inferred from where Dixon personally deploys capital and emphasis. He names no specific tokens — his orientation is sectoral/thesis-driven (open internet, gaming, infra) rather than asset-pick driven — hence empty token lists. No material stance reversals were found; the regulatory concern intensifies through year-end and his Dec 2023 PAC/Fairshake post is the most recent, weighted accordingly. VR/Vision Pro is the one non-crypto enthusiasm and is stale (single June mention).
